description_long: This object is a ceiling-mounted electric fan with three elongated, flat blades radiating from a central circular hub. The blades are a deep reddish-brown color, likely made of painted or coated metal, and are slightly tapered at the ends. The central hub is disc-shaped and appears to be constructed from metal, with a subtle gold accent ring at its base. A vertical rod extends upward from the hub, serving as the mounting shaft for attachment to a ceiling. The fan lacks any visible blade guard or protective grille, leaving the blades fully exposed. No power cord or control panel is visible, suggesting that controls may be integrated into the mounting or operated remotely.
description: A ceiling-mounted electric fan with three reddish-brown metal blades and a central circular hub, featuring a gold accent and exposed blade design.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three blades evenly spaced and the central hub with its gold accent clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the thickness and taper of the blades, as well as the mounting rod extending upward.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the length of the blades and the exposed underside of the central hub.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the alignment of the blades and the attachment point of the mounting rod.
attribute_blade_guard_design: No blade guard is present; the blades are fully exposed with no mesh, grille, or protective covering.
